RNS Number : 6573C
Hammerson PLC
30 April 2026
Hammerson Plc(the 'Company' or 'Hammerson')
Total Voting Rights and Capital
30 April 2026
In compliance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules Rule 5.6.1R, the Company announces that its total issued share capital as at
30 April
2026 consists of
532,054,593
ordinary shares of 5 pence each, of which
8,285
shares are held in treasury.
The total number of voting rights in the Company is therefore
532,046,308
.
This figure should be used by Shareholders as the denominator for calculations by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, ordinary shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules
and/or the Irish Transparency (Directive 2004/109/EC) Regulations 2007, as amended.
For further information contact:
Richard Crowle
Deputy Company Secretary
+44 (0) 20 7887 1000
The announcement above has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.

Ordinary resolution 16 (allotment of shares) and special resolutions 17 and 18 (disapplication of pre-emption rights)
The Board notes that, although resolutions 16, 17 and 18 were passed with the necessary majority, 22.84%, 21.51% and 21.51% of votes cast were against resolutions 16, 17 and 18, respectively. These resolutions are considered routine for listed companies in the UK and are within the Investment Association's share capital management guidelines. Following shareholder consultation, the level of authority sought (and approved by shareholders today) is less than that typically sought by UK listed companies. The Board is aware, however, that certain overseas institutional investors have a policy of not supporting these authorities for the Directors to issue shares and disapply pre-emption rights. The Board considers the flexibility afforded by these authorities to be in the best interests of the Company and shareholders.
In accordance with provision 4 of the UK Corporate Governance Code (the 'Code'), the Board confirms that it will consult and continue to engage with relevant shareholders to understand the reasons behind those votes against. An update will be provided within six months of the AGM, in accordance with the Code, with a final summary to be included in the Company's 2026 annual report and accounts.
Other information
* Special resolution (75% majority required).
** A vote withheld is not a vote in law and is not counted towards the votes cast 'For' or 'Against' a resolution.
(1)
The issued share capital of the Company as at 6.30 pm on Tuesday, 28 April 2026 (the time by which shareholders who wanted to attend, speak and vote at the AGM were entered on the Register) was 532,054,593 ordinary shares, with 132,163 shares held in treasury. The total number of voting rights in Hammerson plc was therefore 531,922,430.
(2)
In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.2, a copy of the resolutions passed, other than the resolutions constituting ordinary business, at the AGM will shortly be available for inspection at the National Storage Mechanism, which is located at
https://www.fca.org.uk/markets/primary-markets/regulatory-disclosures/national-storage-mechanism
.
(3)
A copy of the poll results for the Annual General Meeting is also available on the Hammerson plc website at
https://www.hammerson.com/investors/shareholder-centre/general-meetings
.
(4)
The full text of the resolutions is set out in the Notice of Meeting which is also available at
https://www.hammerson.com/investors/shareholder-centre/general-meetings
.
Richard Crowle
Deputy Company Secretary
This announcement has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.

latest.title: Hammerson CFO to retire
latest.content:
30 Apr 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 4591C
Hammerson PLC
30 April 2026
30 April 2026
Hammerson plc
Hammerson CFO to retire
Hammerson plc ("
Hammerson
" or the "
Company
") today announces that Himanshu Raja has informed the Board of his intention to retire from his position as Chief Financial Officer. He will step down as CFO and as a Director of the Company on 12 August 2026, following publication of the Company's results for the half-year ended 30 June 2026.
After five years as Hammerson's CFO and a career spanning 40 years with 15 years as a listed company CFO, across a multitude of sectors, Himanshu has decided now is the right time for him to retire.
Himanshu will remain employed by the Company for the next 12 months. During this period, he will remain available to assist with an orderly transition. Richard Shaw, Deputy CFO, will become Interim CFO when Himanshu steps down.
The Board will now commence a formal and thorough recruitment process to identify a permanent successor for Himanshu, which will include consideration of internal and external candidates. Further updates will be given in due course.
Rob Wilkinson, Chief Executive Officer of Hammerson, said, "Hammerson's FY25 results are a testament to the hard work and exceptional execution of the Hammerson team over the last five years. Himanshu has been an outstanding CFO over that period, leaving the Group well placed for the next phase of growth. I would like to personally thank him for his support and continued commitment to Hammerson's success."
Himanshu Raja, CFO of Hammerson, said, "When I look back at my time at Hammerson, what stands out for me is the amazing people and the phenomenal progress we have made together. I'm sincerely grateful to my colleagues, the Executive team and the Board for their support throughout my time here. As I retire, I leave with genuine confidence in the future of the business and its continued success."
Robert Noel, Chair of Hammerson, said, "Over the past five years Himanshu has been a valued member of the Hammerson Executive team and Board, supporting the Company's turnaround and transformation and helping to strengthen Hammerson's financial position. On behalf of the Board, I want to thank him for his contribution to Hammerson's success and wish him the very best for the future."
The remuneration arrangements in relation to Himanshu's retirement from the Company, which will be consistent with the Company's Remuneration Policy, will be disclosed on the Company's website at the appropriate time in accordance with section 430(2B) of the Companies Act 2006.
There is no further information to be declared in accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.6.
Ends
For further information contact:
Investors
Josh Warren, Director of Group Performance and
IR
Tel: +44 (0)20 7887
1053
josh.warren@hammerson.com
Media
Tom
Gough,
Head
of
Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 7887
1092
Tom.gough@hammerson.com
Oliver Hughes, Ollie Hoare and Charles Hirst,
MHP
Tel: +44 (0)20 3128
8100
Hammerson@mhpgroup.com
Notes:
1. The Company expects to publish its results for the half-year ended 30 June 2026, on the usual timeline in late July 2026.
2. Richard Shaw will not be appointed as a Director of the Company whilst he serves as Interim CFO.
This announcement has also been released on the SENS system of the Johannesburg Stock Exchange and on Euronext Dublin.

latest.title: Director/PDMR Shareholding
latest.content:
28 Apr 2026 15:00
RNS Number : 2575C
Hammerson PLC
28 April 2026
Hammerson plc (the 'Company')
Notification of Transactions of Persons Discharging Managerial Responsibilities ('PDMRs')
28 April 2026
The award of nil-cost options over ordinary shares of five pence each in the Company ('Shares') granted to Himanshu Raja on 27 April 2021 under the Hammerson plc Restricted Share Scheme (the 'Award'), ceased to be subject to a holding period and became exercisable on 27 April 2026. On 27 April 2026, Himanshu exercised the Award and sold 58,428 Shares to cover his tax and national insurance liability in respect of the Award. Himanshu has retained the remaining 65,450 Shares which were subject to the Award.
This announcement is made in accordance with the requirements of the UK Market Abuse Regulation. The notification of dealing form can be found below.

company_30s.what_they_do: Hammerson plc owns and manages a portfolio of shopping centres across the UK, providing retail space to a mix of high‑traffic anchor tenants and specialty stores.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK retail property sector is a key part of the economy, supplying physical outlets for thousands of brands. Hammerson’s sites sit in major cities and are positioned to benefit from any shift back to in‑person shopping, while its diversified tenant mix cushions against single‑store risk.
company_30s.current_state: The group is in a recovery phase after a steep slide in the last few years. Recent announcements show a change in senior management, a dividend payout, and a new debt programme aimed at refinancing. The company is trading well below its recent peak, suggesting a steep discount to its underlying asset value.

rns_narrative: Management is reshuffling: the CFO is retiring and new directors are taking board seats, signalling a fresh strategic push.
Financially, the firm has just declared a dividend, updated its AGM details, and launched an EMTN programme to refinance debt and fund future acquisitions.
Shareholding shifts are being disclosed, indicating that institutional investors are rebalancing their positions in the REIT.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism – the chatter is sparse but leans toward a rebound once the dividend is paid.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish posts highlight the upcoming dividend, potential upgrades, and a belief that the company has turned a corner after the 2025 results.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish voices worry that the dividend will be small and that retail footfall remains weak, fearing the stock may stay flat or dip further.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted slightly toward optimism after the dividend announcement, but overall engagement remains low.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent interest, giving the stock a high surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: The 2026 AGM and dividend declaration – a cash payout can trigger a short‑term lift.
Completion of the EMTN refinancing, which could improve the balance sheet and allow for new acquisitions or buybacks.
Any announcement of a new tenant lease or a sale of a property could signal renewed confidence in the portfolio.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume spike as the price touches the 52‑week low support, and for a rebound in earnings or rent collections that would confirm a reversal.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early phase of a crash‑recovery cycle. The high APEX compression suggests a reversal is likely, but we need a clear volume or earnings confirmation before committing.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share is trading well below its recent high, creating a large upside window. If the mean reversion thesis plays out, a move back to roughly halfway between the low and the all‑time high would deliver a 70%+ return from the current level, matching the historical success of deep crash signals.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the market stays flat or the underlying retail environment deteriorates further, the stock could remain depressed. The worst realistic scenario would see the share stay near its current level or drop a little more before a longer‑term recovery.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside exceeds 70% while the downside risk is capped at the current level. The asymmetry favors a small capital commitment for a large potential payoff.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter near the 52‑week low support, set a stop just below that level, and size the position to 2–3% of the portfolio. Target the halfway point between the low and the all‑time high, or a 30% rise from the entry if the trend is strong.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a clear volume surge at the support zone or a positive earnings announcement. A sustained move above the low with increasing volume confirms the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risks (e.g., delisting or bankruptcy) are present, so there is no reason to abandon the bullish view.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Hammerson’s deep crash, quiet market, and upcoming dividend and refinancing events create a strong asymmetry that favours a contrarian bullish stance.
